Title: A differentially regulated AP2/ERF transcription factor gene cluster acts downstream of a MAP kinase cascade to modulate terpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis in Catharanthus roseus

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF transcription factor, subgroup IX AP2 DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA inducible; preferentially induced in roots over leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MeJA inducible in seedlings, leaves and especially roots; highly elevated in ORCA4-overexpression hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: tabersonine; ajmalicine; catharanthine; terpenoid indole alkaloids (TIAs)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Includes pharmaceutically important TIAs that serve as precursors to vinblastine/vincristine and other bioactive alkaloids
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strong TIA pathway gene expression and markedly increases alkaloid accumulation, especially tabersonine (>40-fold), as well as ajmalicine and catharanthine in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ASα; TDC; CPR; G10H; IS; LAMT; SLS; STR; ZCT1; ZCT2; ZCT3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Transcriptional activation of multiple TIA biosynthetic genes; also increases expression of ZCT repressors, suggesting a negative feedback loop
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: TIA biosynthesis, including indole and seco-iridoid branches and downstream strictosidine-forming steps
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator with broader target spectrum than ORCA3; sufficient alone to strongly boost TIA accumulation in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Methyl jasmonate; CrMAPKK1-MAPK3/6 cascade; indirect influence from CrMYC2
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Induces ZCT1/ZCT2/ZCT3 repressors, indicating negative feedback/fine-tuning
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CrMAPK3 (functional enhancement inferred); CrMAPKK1 pathway upstream
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter for overexpression in hairy roots and protoplast effectors
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 μM methyl jasmonate treatment; hairy root overexpression context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hairy root overexpression; qRT-PCR; promoter transactivation assay in tobacco protoplasts; LC-ESI-MS/MS metabolite profiling; co-expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Upregulation of ZCT repressors suggests compensatory negative regulation; no growth penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORCA4 is a MeJA-inducible AP2/ERF clustered with ORCA3 and ORCA5. 2. ORCA4 activates TDC, CPR and STR promoters in protoplasts. 3. ORCA4 overexpression upregulates ASα, TDC, CPR, G10H, IS, LAMT, SLS and STR in hairy roots. 4. ORCA4 overexpression strongly increases tabersonine, ajmalicine and catharanthine accumulation. 5. ORCA4 has overlapping but broader regulatory scope than ORCA3.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for boosting medicinal TIA production; especially promising for hairy root, cell culture, or synthetic biology platforms, potentially combined with upstream JA/MAPK regulators or pathway enzymes.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF transcription factor, subgroup IX AP2 DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA inducible; preferentially induced in roots
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MeJA inducible in seedlings, leaves and roots
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive specialized metabolites including anticancer alkaloid pathway end products
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a positive transactivator of selected TIA pathway promoters, but in this paper no direct metabolite overproduction phenotype in stable hairy roots was shown
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Transcriptional activation of STR strongly and CPR/TDC moderately in protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator within the ORCA cluster, but less strongly established than ORCA4 for in planta metabolite enhancement
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Methyl jasmonate; indirect influence from CrMYC2; CrMAPKK1-MAPK3/6 cascade
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CrMAPKK1 pathway upstream; likely CrMAPK3/6 functional enhancement inferred
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; promoter transactivation assay in tobacco protoplasts; co-expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORCA5 is a MeJA-inducible AP2/ERF gene physically clustered with ORCA3 and ORCA4. 2. ORCA5 activates STR, CPR and TDC promoters in transient assays, with strongest effect on STR. 3. CrMYC2 does not directly activate the ORCA5 promoter, but ORCA5 transcript rises in CrMYC2-overexpression hairy roots, implying indirect regulation. 4. ORCA5 is part of the MAPK-responsive ORCA regulatory module.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful auxiliary regulator for combinatorial engineering of TIAs, though direct in planta metabolite gain evidence is weaker than for ORCA4.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF transcription factor, subgroup IX AP2 DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly MeJA inducible; induced more in roots than leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MeJA-induced in seedlings, leaves and especially roots
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal alkaloids including vinblastine/vincristine pathway intermediates
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates multiple TIA genes and acts with CrMYC2 and MAPK signaling, but unlike ORCA4 prior ORCA3 overexpression alone did not increase TIA accumulation in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Direct transcriptional activation; synergistic co-activation with CrMYC2 on TDC and CPR
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: TIA biosynthesis, particularly indole pathway and downstream TIA steps
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Established JA-responsive key regulator of TIA genes, but not sufficient alone for strong alkaloid enhancement in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Methyl jasmonate; direct activation by CrMYC2; enhancement by CrMAPKK1-MAPK3/6 cascade
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of feedforward JA-responsive cascade downstream of CrMYC2; subject to MAPK-mediated post-translational activation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Co-regulatory interaction with CrMYC2 on target promoters
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter transactivation assay; T/G-box mutagenesis; co-expression analysis; site-directed mutagenesis of phosphorylation motifs; prior hairy root overexpression evidence discussed
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression alone insufficient to increase TIA accumulation in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORCA3 is a MeJA-responsive AP2/ERF regulator of TIA biosynthesis. 2. It activates STR, TDC and CPR promoters. 3. CrMYC2 directly activates the ORCA3 promoter and synergizes with ORCA3 on TDC and CPR. 4. CrMAPK3 enhances ORCA3 transactivation, and mutation of ORCA3 phosphorylation sites reduces this enhancement. 5. ORCA3 function overlaps with but is narrower than ORCA4.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important component for stacked engineering with CrMYC2 and MAPK regulators rather than as a standalone target.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor; jasmonate-responsive MYC family
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in transgenic hairy roots; co-expressed with ORCA4 and TIA genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates ORCA3 directly and co-regulates TDC and CPR with ORCA3, while indirectly increasing ORCA4/5 expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ORCA3; TDC; CPR; indirect positive effect on ORCA4; ORCA5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Direct transcriptional activation via T/G-box binding to ORCA3 and TDC/CPR promoters; synergistic co-activation with ORCA3
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA-responsive transcriptional control of TIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key upstream JA-signaling regulator that links jasmonate perception to ORCA-centered TIA transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedforward activation of ORCA3 plus direct co-regulation of pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Co-regulatory interaction with ORCA3 on TDC and CPR promoters
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter for overexpression in hairy roots and protoplast effectors
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hairy root overexpression; promoter transactivation assay; promoter mutagenesis; dominant repressor fusion (CrMYC2-SRDX); qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomic analysis of CrMYC2-overexpression hairy roots; SRA030483 used for co-expression context
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Indirect induction of unknown intermediary regulator(s); no direct activation of STR promoter
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrMYC2 strongly activates the ORCA3 promoter but not ORCA4/5 promoters directly. 2. CrMYC2-overexpression raises ORCA4 and ORCA5 transcripts indirectly. 3. CrMYC2 directly activates TDC and CPR through T/G-box-related promoter elements. 4. Co-expression with ORCA3 synergistically enhances TDC and CPR promoter activation. 5. Dominant repressor CrMYC2-SRDX suppresses TDC activation, supporting direct promoter binding.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong upstream engineering target for coordinated JA-responsive TIA pathway activation, especially in stacked designs with ORCA4 or MAPK cascade components.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MAP kinase kinase (MAPKK/MKK), serine/threonine protein kinase with kinase interaction motif (KIM) and N-terminal Leu/Pro-rich region
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA inducible; stronger induction in roots than leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MeJA inducible in seedlings and enriched in roots; mildly overexpressed in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: tabersonine; ajmalicine; catharanthine; terpenoid indole alkaloids (TIAs)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts upstream of ORCA cluster and CrMYC2 to enhance TIA gene expression and increase tabersonine, ajmalicine and catharanthine accumulation in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CrMAPK3; CrMAPK6; indirectly ORCA3; ORCA4; ORCA5; CrMYC2; TDC; CPR; IS; STR; ZCTs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Post-translational activation via kinase cascade; enhances transactivation activity of ORCAs and CrMYC2; overexpression increases TIA pathway transcript abundance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Post-translational control of TIA biosynthesis through MAPK signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key upstream signaling node connecting JA response to stronger ORCA/CrMYC2 function and elevated TIA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in MAPK cascade with CrMAPK3/6; KIM-dependent kinase docking
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter for overexpression and protoplast effectors
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hairy root overexpression; qRT-PCR; LC-ESI-MS/MS; autophosphorylation assay; subcellular localization; plant cell two-hybrid assay; KIM mutagenesis; transient promoter activation assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Upregulates ZCT repressors along with pathway genes; no developmental penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrMAPKK1 is a MeJA-inducible nuclear MAPKK clustered in co-expression with TIA regulators and pathway genes. 2. It is an active autophosphorylating kinase. 3. It interacts with CrMAPK3 and CrMAPK6 in plant cells, and its KIM/N-terminus is required for efficient interaction. 4. CrMAPKK1 enhances ORCA3/4/5 and CrMYC2 transactivation of TIA gene promoters. 5. CrMAPKK1 overexpression in hairy roots elevates TIA gene expression and increases tabersonine, ajmalicine and catharanthine accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising upstream signaling engineering target to amplify multiple TIA regulators simultaneously; useful in stacked metabolic engineering but should be balanced to avoid broad signaling side effects.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with CrMAPKK1 and TIA genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances ORCA3 and ORCA4 activation of STR promoter and likely phosphorylates ORCA3 to promote TIA gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Post-translational enhancement of ORCA transactivation through phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: MAPK-mediated post-translational activation of TIA transcription factors
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important signaling component that boosts activity of ORCA regulators rather than directly controlling promoter DNA binding itself
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: plant cell two-hybrid assay; promoter transactivation assay; ORCA3 phosphorylation-site mutagenesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrMAPK3 interacts with CrMAPKK1 in plant cells. 2. Co-expression with ORCA3 or ORCA4 increases STR promoter activation. 3. Mutation of putative ORCA3 MAPK phosphorylation sites reduces CrMAPK3-mediated enhancement. 4. CrMAPK3 is therefore implicated in phosphorylation-based activation of ORCA TFs in TIA regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pathway amplifier for stacked signaling-based engineering of TIA production.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MAP kinase, serine/threonine protein kinase with conserved kinase domain and C-terminal MAPKK docking site
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely acts with CrMAPKK1 as part of the upstream MAPK cascade modulating TIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Likely ORCA3; ORCA4; ORCA5; CrMYC2 via cascade context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Likely phosphorylation-mediated enhancement within the MAPK cascade; direct downstream TF targets not individually demonstrated here
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: MAPK signaling control of TIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supported as a cascade component interacting with CrMAPKK1, but direct metabolic causality is less developed than for CrMAPKK1/CrMAPK3
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S/MMV-based constructs in protoplast interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: plant cell two-hybrid assay; sequence/phylogenetic analysis; co-expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrMAPK6 was cloned as a likely AtMAPK6 ortholog. 2. It interacts with CrMAPKK1 in plant cells. 3. The paper places CrMAPK6 in the CrMAPKK1-MAPK3/6 cascade acting upstream of TIA regulators. 4. Direct metabolite effects of isolated CrMAPK6 manipulation were not tested.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate supportive kinase for future stacked engineering, but requires stronger direct functional validation before deployment.
